author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
We Have No Memory of Our Creation.
title: We Have No Memory of Our Creation.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: And whosoever shall have attempted to fathom such knowledge may not improperly have addressed to him the words we have before quoted, “Seek not out the things that are too high for thee, neither search the things that are above thy strength.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Now it is not a question of mere altitude, such as is beyond our stature, but it is an elevation which our intelligence cannot reach, and a strength which our mental power cannot cope with.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: And yet it is neither the heaven of heavens, nor the measure of the stars, nor the scope of sea and land, nor the nethermost hell; it is our own selves that we are incapable of comprehending; it is our own selves, who, in our too great height and strength, transcend the humble limits of our own knowledge; it is our own selves, whom we are incapable of embracing, although we are certainly not beside ourselves.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: But we are not to be compared with cattle simply because we do not perfectly discover what we ourselves are: and yet you think that we deserve the humiliating comparison, if we have forgotten what we were, even though we knew it once.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: My soul is not now being derived from my parents, is not now receiving insufflation from God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Whichever of these two processes He used, He used when He created me; He is not at this moment using it of me, or within me.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: It is past and gone,—not a present thing, nor a recent one to me.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: I do not even know whether I was aware of it and then forgot it; or whether I was unable, even at the time when it was done, to feel and to know it.
